summary: React.lazy() implementation for CVE Management Dashboard tabs verified - All 14 lazy-loaded tabs load correctly without errors. OverviewTab remains eagerly loaded. Suspense fallback configured properly.

minor.issue: HTTP 500 errors on PolicyEngineTab load - not related to lazy loading, pre-existing API issue

critical_code_review_comments: React.lazy() correctly implemented for all 14 non-default tabs
OverviewTab remains eagerly loaded for instant first paint - correct approach
Suspense boundary with TabFallback component properly wraps lazy components
Named exports correctly handled via .then(m => ({ default: m.ComponentName }))
Users & RBAC tab properly hidden when user lacks 'users.view' permission
context_for_next_testing_agent: React.lazy() code splitting verified. All lazy-loaded tabs (CVE Database, Scanners, Remediation, Governance, SLA Tracker, Reporting, Notifications, Services, SBOM, CI/CD, Infrastructure, Policy Engine, SLA Policies, Audit Trail) load correctly. Users & RBAC tab hidden for non-admin users as expected. Rapid tab switching tested without crashes.

overview_tab.note: Default tab loaded immediately on page load

users_tab.reason: User role 'Analyst' lacks 'users.view' permission - expected RBAC behavior

lazy_loading_tests.suspense_fallback: TabFallback component with Loader2 spinner configured correctly
lazy_loading_tests.rapid_switching_test: PASS - No crashes when switching tabs rapidly

console_errors_detected: HTTP 500 from PolicyEngineTab API calls - pre-existing backend issue, not related to lazy loading
